## [00:00 - 03:00]

What's going on guys? In this video, I really want to talk about stalling points that you will find yourself at when it comes to e-commerce and dropshipping in general. Now, the first stalling point that I really came to about a year and a half ago was not being able to just be profitable. That was literally the first thing. I couldn't find the right type of product with the right Facebook ads and I lost quite a bit of money during that time as well. On Facebook ads, I actually got emotionally attached to a product, something one of my mentors, Fred Lam says, Never to do is to get emotionally attached to a product. You think that it's going to sell so you keep pushing it, trying different Facebook ads. If you guys can't make things work within like the 20, you know, first ad sets you create and within the first 72 hours of testing all those ad sets, if it's not working, just cut it and move on to the next product. That was the first really pivotal point in my business was going broke with e-commerce and dropshipping. I know a lot of people have been there and feel that pain and it really just comes down to not testing enough. So don't get emotionally attached. Make sure you guys just keep testing and test with small amounts. You can test with small budgets. You know, test with $5. You don't have to put 50 bucks out there per ad set to start. Just do that once you start scaling. Now, the second really pivotal point in my business was when I hit a plateau in my sales, something a lot of people do as well. You know, you start kind of capping out daily at a certain number, whether that be, you know, $500, $1,000, $2,000. I usually find myself capping out, you know, around $3,000 to $4,000 or $5,000. And it gets a little difficult for me to scale up Facebook-wise, you know, through my Facebook ads. So I'm going to start talking about a few certain apps that you guys can use to increase your average order value. When it comes to actually hitting those caps with your sales, a big thing that you guys can do instead of just trying to push more money into Facebook ads, is actually creating, you know, better offers for people to buy. Bigger offers, upsells, bundles. So there's a few really critical apps inside Shopify that have made me a lot of money, you know, more money than you have to actually pay monthly for. Now, the first app you guys can see right here on my screen, it's called Boost Sales slash Upsell and Cross-Sell. If I just click on this, so the reason I really like this app right here is there's so many features when it comes to upsells and cross-sells, and it really has a good layout. So the cool thing is you'll see like seven options of different products people can buy. If you guys use just like a basic upsell, you're only going to be able to add one product in there, but this one they can see options. And when you're doing upsells for products, you really want to think about what goes hand in hand with what you're already hooking them in with. Let's just say you're hooking them in with like a bracelet that costs $20. Well, then you could upsell them a necklace that looks very similar, maybe has the same design, and you upsell to them for $30 or $40. And just having a certain percentage of people actually buy that will increase your average order value,

## [03:00 - 06:03]

ultimately allowing you to make more money daily without having to spend any extra money on Facebook ads. So that was something that I hit about six months ago.
